metadata.title:
STATE FEDERATION VICE PRESIDENT


ON TAFT AS FRIEND OF UNION LABOR

pages.raw_text:
Editor, The Press-I would like
to ask the Spokesman-Review a
few questions on matters in which
it is trying to mislead the public
by political lies and to explain
some of those lies.


The Review says everyone knows
where to find Taft on the injunc-
ton issue; he stands now where
he always did. Through a Roose-
welt statement the Review also
says Taft is the best friend labor
ever had and all union men should
vote for him.


Let's analyze this a little. When
| was president of the Spokane La
Dor council the council endorsed a
protest sent to Taft, the secretary
of war, against military bands COM
peting with bandsmen whose liv-
ing depended upon their playing.
Noone ever heard of a reply to
this from the great friend of la
bor." Practically all the unions
Joined in the protest. A poll of the
musicians shows that less than
five per cent are going to vote for
Taft. They have a membership of
$4,000. Why doesn't the Review
print that?


The Review says Taft favors the
Yukon exposition to be held in Sc
attic, benefit for laboring men
that need the work. The fact is
that every union in the state of
Washington has that exposition on
the unfair list and has protested
through Gompers against the gov
eminent making an appropriation
until the exposition management
lives up toits agreement with
unions on the coast. Labors friend,
Taft, favors the exposition.


The Review says Taft wes made
an honorary member of the Steam
Shovelmen's union and would have
the public believe Taft to be the
only one so favored AS matter
of fact, six congressman, three re-
publicans and three democrats,
shared the same honor. Here is
how T. J. Dolan, general secretary
treasurer of the shovelmen, ex-
